game_name: Battletoads & Double Dragon: The Ultimate Team

sub_pixel_system.notes: No sub-pixel system data documented for Genesis version

special_mechanics.name: Rope Swinging
special_mechanics.description: Players can hang on and swing across ropes/cables with special attacks

physics_values.cable_bash_attack: Wrecking-ball-like charge while hanging on cable
physics_values.rope_kick_attack: Kicks while hanging on rope (Swingin' Size Thirteens)

special_mechanics.name: Ledge Hanging
special_mechanics.description: New ability to hang off and move across sides of platforms with kicking mechanics

physics_values.fall_condition: Player can fall off and lose a life if other enemies step on fingers
physics_values.ledge_kick_attack: Back 'N Front Punt kick while hanging on ledge

special_mechanics.name: Speeder Bike Riding
special_mechanics.description: Turbo bike sections with special attacks

physics_values.bike_attack: Backward kicks while riding speeder bike (Bikin' Bash)

special_mechanics.name: Character-Specific Abilities
special_mechanics.description: Battletoads have unique smash hit attacks; Lee twins have Dragon Force techniques

battletoads_attacks: Take Out the Trash (pick-up and throw)
No Way Back Thwack (walker leg bashes)

tier_notes: Tier 3: No precise physics parameters documented. Wikipedia source provides gameplay mechanics and character abilities but lacks quantitative movement data. 16-bit versions (Genesis, SNES) have identical gameplay to NES counterpart with two-button control scheme for attacks and jumping.
